tavily.answer: The latest conflict, security, and humanitarian developments in Mexico indicate a significant displacement crisis driven by cartel warfare. In Guerrero state, attacks have led to the emptying of villages, forcing families like María Cabrera’s to flee with minimal belongings. This phenomenon has been described as an invisible crisis with long-term humanitarian consequences, as there are few official figures on the number of displaced people, who often have no resources once they leave. The number of internal displacements caused by violence or conflict reached a record high in 2025, surpassing those caused by disasters for the first time, according to the Internal Displacement Monitoring Centre.

items.title: Mexico City tightens security and caps crowds for Mexico-England match after 4 deaths - The Washington Post
items.snippet: Mexico is doubling security and limiting capacity at the “Angel of Independence” monument and a fan festival in Mexico City for the Mexico-England World Cup match on Sunday. Mexican soccer fans display a banner that reads in Spanish “The ball comeback to home”
